steps.title: Identify what role 'which sites' plays
steps.content: The phrase “which sites to add next to the growing registry” is the direct object of “select”—it identifies which items the board gathers to select.

steps.title: Apply the rule for verb-object connections
steps.content:
A verb must connect directly to its object without separating punctuation.

Choice D does this, placing “select” directly next to “which sites” without any punctuation between them.

A, B, and C each disrupt this connection by inserting a comma, colon, or semicolon.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
